properties.remark: 25 FEET BY 30 FEET SHED ANCHORED 3 FEET INTO GROUND...TOTALLY RIPPED OUT AND IMPALLED INTO A FENCE AND A ROOF OF NEIGHBORING HOUSE

NWS STORM SURVEY CONCLUDED THAT DAMAGING STRAIGHT LINE WINDS ESTIMATED AT 65-70 MPH IMPACTED THE TOWN OF ORANGE GROVE. THE DAMAGE PATH WAS 4.5 MILES LONG AND 3.2 MILES WIDE. APPROXIMATELY 500-600 HOMES SUFFERED MINOR- MODERATE DAMAGE WITH A FEW BROKEN WINDOWS...DAMAGED SHINGLES OR SIDING. A FEW HOMES SUFFERED MAJOR DAMAGE WITH ALL WEST FACING WINDOWS BROKEN...AND EXTERIOR SIDING DESTROYED BY WIND BLOWN HAIL. LARGEST HAIL SIZE WAS ESTIMATED AT 2 INCHES.

GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL THROUGH TOWN OF ORANGE GROVE. THE HAIL WAS WIND DRIVEN AND BROKE WINDOWS ON SOME HOMES AND DAMAGED SIDING. SEVERAL TREES KNOCKED DOWN. ALSO REPORTS OF DAMAGE TO SOME BUILDINGS NORTH OF ORANGE GROVE IN THE AREA OF NORTH FM 78, JENNIFER LOOP, AND CR 303.
A SECTION OF THE ROOF OF A MOBILE HOME WAS BLOWN OFF BY HIGH WINDS ON SWEET WATER ROAD.
ESTIMATED GOLF BALL SIZED HAIL THROUGH TOWN OF ORANGE GROVE.

NWS STORM SURVEY AND LOCAL VIDEO CONCLUDED EF-0 TORNADO WITH WINDS ESTIMATED AT 65 MPH BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN IN NORTH REFUGIO NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF W HEARD ST AND 800 BLOCK OF NORTH ALAMO ST. THE TORNADO REMOVED THE ROOF AND COLLAPSED THE NORTH FACING EXTERIOR WALL OF ONE MOBILE HOME. MINOR SHINGLE DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED ON A COUPLE HOMES ALONG E HOUSTON ST...AND SEVERAL LARGE TREES WERE TOPPLED. THE TORNADO WAS ESTIMATED AT 50 YARDS WIDE AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR ONE THIRD OF A MILE.
